title: Non-ANSI port declarations ignore default_nettype none
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
A Verilog file containing

```
    `default_nettype none
    module somename(i_clk, o_out);
        input i_clk;
        output o_out;
    endmodule
```

Yosys script:

```
    read -formal somename.v
    prep -top somename
```

## Expected behavior

The code above should generate an error.  According to the SV spec, 23.2.2.3, the type of variable for both i_clk and o_out should be given by default_nettype, and should therefore be "none".  Hence, yosys should create an error from this data.

## Actual behavior

Instead, these values are interpreted as nets (wires).

## Side note

I was actually trying to chase down a different error, whereby o_out was given a definition in its declaration, as in:

    output o_out = toggle;

    reg toggle;
    always @(posedge i_clk)
        toggle <= !toggle;

This appears to be a non-standard behavior, but I can't seem to find the description of how this should be handled with the SV spec, hence I'm reporting on the first error listed above.

Dan
